name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: Things have taken a dark, disturbing turn in this election
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Today's an important day to say you're with her | Friend --

We’ve taken a dark, disturbing turn in this election.

You’ve seen it firsthand: The Republican convention has descended into chaos, with bigoted ranting from its extremist speakers and hate-filled screams of “Lock her up!” And Donald Trump hasn’t even given his acceptance speech yet.

On Tuesday night, there was bizarre talk of a connection between Hillary and Lucifer. On Wednesday, a Trump advisor called for Hillary to be executed. This madness has to stop. And it has to stop now.

It's time to step up and stand with Hillary. She is strong enough to take whatever comes her way -- **[but your support is what will take us across the finish line and ensure that Donald Trump and the party he hijacked get nowhere near the White House.](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MjYwNDQ3OTkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQzMDk1NjM1S0&mt=1&rt=0)**

Not now. Not ever.

**This is our country. And we won’t be insulted, attacked, and bullied out of it. Show our strength now -- chip in and we’ll send you a free sticker as a thank you for your support:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjYwNDQ3OTkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQzMDk1NjM1S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you,

Huma

Huma Abedin
Vice Chair
Hillary for America

She's been relentless in fighting for everyday Hoosiers and everyone in the office wants to make sure she knows how much we appreciate her hard work. But before we hand over her birthday card, we wanted to make sure you had a chance to add your name too! **[Will you sign Christina's birthday card? body:
After four straight days of anger | Friend --

It’s been a long week in Cleveland -- four straight days of anger, and Trump’s speech last night doubled down on it all.

Some people may be feeling discouraged about what the Republican convention showed us about our country.

Don't be.

On Monday, our own convention begins, where we’ll reveal our values. We’ve built the most progressive Democratic platform in history, which calls for affordable health care, affordable college, a fair wage, strengthening LGBT, labor, and reproductive rights, criminal justice reform, and the explicit acknowledgment that black lives do matter.

Be proud. It’s going to be inspiring, unifying, and by this time next week, you may just have recovered from what happened in Cleveland.

**We’ll have to work our hearts out not to let this country, and all the progress we’ve worked for, fall into the hands of Donald Trump and the Republicans. But we need your help to get it done. Chip in to get your free sticker, and let us know that you're in:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MjYwNDU3MTgS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQzMTI1MjMzS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you,

Hillary

Friend,

This week Mitch McConnell made it clear what he's got planned for our country if we don't take back the Senate -- and it's scary.

Right off the bat, he promised that Senate Republicans (like Pat Toomey) will continue their outrageous blockade of the Supreme Court by refusing to give Judge Merrick Garland a fair vote:

We've fought hard and now we're neck-and-neck with Pat Toomey -- and we need to show that we've got the grassroots strength to pull ahead. If we rally hard now to beat Toomey and take away Mitch McConnell's Senate majority, then all his dangerous plans come tumbling down.

name: Corry
subject: what a week
body:
| Peter --

At the RNC Convention in Cleveland this week, Rob [celebrated our team's 500 summer interns](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Frobportman%2Fstatus%2F755406897310670848&e=b7b7d93df9a6acebcc81bd31cc7e75b1&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=convention_fr&n=1&test_email=1&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=convention_fr&n=1) with Newt Gingrich and Joni Ernst, [kayaked with wounded warriors](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Frobportman%2Fstatus%2F755786958085820416&e=b7b7d93df9a6acebcc81bd31cc7e75b1&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=convention_fr&n=2&test_email=1&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=convention_fr&n=2) on the Cuyahoga River, helped build a Habitat for Humanity house, [hosted over 1,000 Ohio Republicans](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Frobportman%2Fstatus%2F756202281108811776&e=b7b7d93df9a6acebcc81bd31cc7e75b1&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=convention_fr&n=3&test_email=1&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=convention_fr&n=3) at the Great Lakes Science Center, and made stops across the city to show off Cleveland.

[](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fcontribute.robportman.com%2Fgrassroots_fund%3Fcampaign_id%3D40239cbc958426f8ecf957b430ee38d3%26email%3Ddpwillis67%40gmail.com%26utm_campaign%3Dconvention_fr&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=convention_fr&n=4 "Donate Here!")
Where was Ted Strickland? The _only_ thing he did in his _one_ public appearance this week was get critized for a failed campaign stunt that was labeled as racially insensitive. When he was facing tough questions about his offensive actions, the media reported he "was then pulled away by his staff before he could answer any more questions."

Ted's hiding and hoping the National Democrats will bail out his campaign that has no money, no grassroots support and no message.

But there's bad news: **Harry Reid's Super PAC has spent $10 million attacking Rob on television across Ohio**. And he's showing no signs of stopping.
That's why we are building an unrivaled grassroots organization that surpassed 2.9 million voter contacts just last night. Will you help us fight back and fund our grassroots program?

Friend, Yesterday, we got news that the NBA is moving the All-Star game out of Charlotte as a direct result of Governor McCrory's HB2. That means our hotel workers, our restaurants, our small business owners -- the everyday folks who make our state go -- will lose out on another $100 million because of this law. **This is devastating news. North Carolinians simply cannot afford Governor McCrory's lack of leadership anymore.** **[Chip in today to help us defeat him in November.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202650/2fde7c66/4075e4c9/156a6247/971391083/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cooper-hb2allstargame?utm_campaign=fnd_allstr_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&refcode=em16_160722_fnd_gq_allstr_ful__fp&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")** | | [**Chip in ->**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202650/2fde7c66/4075e4c9/156a6247/971391083/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cooper-hb2allstargame?utm_campaign=fnd_allstr_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&refcode=em16_160722_fnd_gq_allstr_ful__fp&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")

This isn't just about a basketball game. This is about who we are as a state and where we're going. Are we going to be a state that discriminates against our own? Are we going to be a state that jeopardizes our economy because of political ideology?
Apparently, that's who Governor McCrory wants us to be. It doesn't have to be this way. We know -- Roy knows -- that we are not this.
**[We cannot sit on the sidelines and let Governor McCrory take us down this path. Chip in today.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202650/2fde7c66/4075e4c9/156a6247/971391083/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cooper-hb2allstargame?utm_campaign=fnd_allstr_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&refcode=em16_160722_fnd_gq_allstr_ful__fp&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")**

| Greetings,
---
| As we get down to the final few weeks before the August 9 primary, Phil and Team Scott are working incredibly hard to ensure Vermonters understand what’s at stake in this election and why Phil is the right leader for Vermont. Early voting is going on now, so don’t forget to **_VOTE EARLY FOR PHIL_**! A few of this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign are below.

**THE ISSUES MATTER! LEARN MORE ABOUT PHIL’S PROPOSALS**
Phil has spent his entire career as a public servant and small business owner connecting with people to ensure he hears all perspectives and understands an issue from the inside out. The issues can often get lost in an election – and we all know our opponent is working to distract us from those issues with misleading ads and statements. But **Phil is working hard to make certain you know his plans for improving our economy, his approach to leadership and his view on critical issues in Vermont.**

Please take a moment to explore, and share, Phil’s proposals on strengthening the economy, rebuilding our workforce, and getting our fiscal house in order. **Don’t be fooled by an opponent who’s desperately trying to mislead voters and twist Phil’s record and positions. See for yourself:**

* [Truth in Budgeting & Real Fiscal Responsibility](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=df1ffe2322&e=19cebfe173)
* [Government Modernization: Efficiency through Innovation](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=75a717963d&e=19cebfe173)
* [Restoring Faith & Trust in Government](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=7bbcdeff1c&e=19cebfe173)

**_Learn more_** about Phil’s [PRINCIPLES](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e78f354a54&e=19cebfe173), [POSITIONS](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=0c728ba3b1&e=19cebfe173), and [LEADERSHIP](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=394dbe82b2&e=19cebfe173) at [www.philscott.org](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=952b0ffdc1&e=19cebfe173).
***
**PHIL RESPONDS TO DISAPPOINTING STATE REVENUE, OFFERS HIS SOLUTIONS**
Late last week, the Shumlin administration announced the General Fund revenues grew by only 2.3% last fiscal year – growth that would have been essentially zero if not for $30 million in new taxes paid by Vermonters. **Clearly, the state is not budgeting sustainably and _as Governor, Phil will not allow this trend to continue_.**

As Phil said in a statement this week, “**I firmly believe we cannot raise more taxes – we do not have that capacity.** That’s why my administration will focus on growing the economy and **I will not propose or sign a budget that grows faster than the economy or wages** in the previous year.” Read the full statement [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=cb4c48d6f6&e=19cebfe173).

Following an Emergency Board Meeting that provided more detail on the revenue forecast, Phil again condemned the budgeting practices of the current administration. “This repeated pattern of spending more than Vermonters can afford, raising taxes and acting surprised when we are faced with shortfalls year after year is a clear sign that **Vermont’s system of budgeting is broken**.”

“In reality, the state’s revenue projections are hypotheticals,” he continued. “They are based on the best guess of the state’s economists. Instead of building budgets based on hypothetical, frequently inaccurate projections, **we need to build state budgets off clear, verifiable economic data.”** Read the full statement [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=8015452797&e=19cebfe173) and find more details on Phil’s budget policy and proposals [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=f4d9a0ec13&e=19cebfe173).
***
**A LEADER TO BE PROUD OF: PHIL RELEASES FIRST TV AD**
We’re taking Phil’s positive message to the airwaves, releasing the campaign’s first TV ad this week. Phil’s message highlights the values and priorities of his campaign for Governor, stressing his commitment to “make Vermont more affordable, the economy stronger and political campaigns an example for our children.”

Phil tells Vermonters, “I’m the candidate who knows what it’s like to live paycheck-to-paycheck while raising a family and working hard to grow a business and create jobs — right here in Vermont.”

View the official press release and full advertisement [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=3b0af1d373&e=19cebfe173). As Phil says in the video – if you want to do things the “Vermont Way,” join us. Find out how to get involved [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9df93bef29&e=19cebfe173).
| [](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TTQxSn-t3zQ)
---

***

**MEETING & GREETING THROUGHOUT VERMONT**
Team Scott had another busy week, connecting with Vermonters to discuss how Phil can help make Vermont more affordable and run his campaign the Vermont Way. See a few fun photos from this week’s events below, including **St. Albans’ Kingman Street Klassic, Lyndonville’s Stars & Stripes Parade, the Essex Junction Block Party, Lamoille County Field Days, and the VCRD/VPT gubernatorial forum**.

Photo credit: @PhilScott4VT

Missed Team Scott this week? We hope to see you next week at events, including the Burlington [Rotary Club Q&A](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4ead35bc75&e=19cebfe173) and for a Meet and Greet on [Lake Bomoseen](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=fe2a635d4b&e=19cebfe173). View our Events page [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=1e430e2860&e=19cebfe173) for all upcoming events.

***

**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

**_Phil: It’s Time for Truth in Budgeting_**
“This repeated pattern of spending more than Vermonters can afford, raising taxes and acting surprised when we are faced with shortfalls year after year is a clear sign that Vermont’s system of budgeting is broken.” (Source: Times Argus: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4aabf7a7fe&e=19cebfe173))

**_Scott Fiscally Responsible_**
**“** I’m voting for Phil Scott. He’s got the experience in government and pragmatic experience of a business owner who knows how these expensive experiments are costing Vermonters.” _Lea Hatch, Northfield_(Source: Times Argus: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=78eaf2668a&e=19cebfe173))

**_Scott Good for Bennington County and Vermont_**
“Scott wants to get Vermont back on track and growing again. He is ready to tackle affordability issues around housing, education and the cost of living. A veteran Legislator known for his decency, fairness and consensus building skills, he creates common ground in the Statehouse to get things done. A small business owner, he understands firsthand that sector's pressures.” _Liz Ruffa, East Dorset_(Source: Manchester Journal: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=d49974a600&e=19cebfe173))

***

**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**

**_Burlington Free Press: Vermont aging faster than the rest of the U.S._**
Burlington Free Press Columnist and UVM Associate Professor of Economics Art Woolf analyzed new data this week indicating that Vermont’s population is aging more quickly than the rest of the U.S. Woolf noted that Vermont’s median age is now 42.8 – rising by 10 years since 1990, while the national median has risen only five years during that same time. Read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=3365c93304&e=19cebfe173).

Phil has made it clear that solving our demographic challenges is critical to growing our economy. That’s why his plan to grow the economy focuses on rebuilding the working-age population so we can grow our workforce and reduce our individual tax burden. Read that plan [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=2814127dee&e=19cebfe173).

**_WCAX: Vt. Kids learn police tactics from the pros_**
Phil often talks about a civic duty and our shared responsibility to give back to society in whatever way speaks to us most – whether that’s through public service, military service, law enforcement or emergency response, community building, volunteering, etc. Educating our kids on the opportunities for fulfilling that civic duty is more important than ever. WCAX highlighted a unique program from the Northfield Police Department – a great example of how we can engage our youth and give them a sense of service. Read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=56f531e6e0&e=19cebfe173) and watch Phil discuss civic duty [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e1542f13d6&e=19cebfe173).

*******

**Phil and Team Scott are working very hard to share Phil’s positive messages across the state. If you like what our campaign stands for,**[please consider making a donation today!](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=389a69fa2b&e=19cebfe173)**Just last week it was reported that our Primary opponent is well on his way to spending $2,000,000 dollars – of his own money - and he’s using that money on misleading, dishonest, negative ads. Please help Phil and the team run a positive campaign, one that our children can be proud of!**

[DONATE TODAY!](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=82f4be34f0&e=19cebfe173)

*******

Thanks to everyone that came out to events and participated in meetings this week. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities everyday – and share your thoughts – [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ba341d5bf8&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=b82d505c3b&e=19cebfe173).

Sincerely,
Team Scott

Phil’s message highlights the values and priorities of his campaign for Governor, stressing his commitment to “make Vermont more affordable, the economy stronger and political campaigns an example for our children.” Phil tells Vermonters, “I’m the candidate who knows what it’s like to live paycheck-to-paycheck while raising a family and working hard to grow a business and create jobs — right here in Vermont.” View the official press release and full advertisement . As Phil says in the video – if you want to do things the “Vermont Way,” join us. Find out how to get involved . | MEETING & GREETING THROUGHOUT VERMONT Team Scott had another busy week, connecting with Vermonters to discuss how Phil can help make Vermont more affordable and run his campaign the Vermont Way. See a few fun photos from this week’s events below, including St. Albans’ Kingman Street Klassic, Lyndonville’s Stars & Stripes Parade, the Essex Junction Block Party, Lamoille County Field Days, and the VCRD/VPT gubernatorial forum. Photo credit: @PhilScott4VT Missed Team Scott this week? We hope to see you next week at events, including the Burlington and for a Meet and Greet on . View our Events page for all upcoming events. ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS Phil: It’s Time for Truth in Budgeting “This repeated pattern of spending more than Vermonters can afford, raising taxes and acting surprised when we are faced with shortfalls year after year is a clear sign that Vermont’s system of budgeting is broken.” (Source: Times Argus: ) Scott Fiscally Responsible “ I’m voting for Phil Scott. He’s got the experience in government and pragmatic experience of a business owner who knows how these expensive experiments are costing Vermonters.” Lea Hatch, Northfield(Source: Times Argus: ) Scott Good for Bennington County and Vermont “Scott wants to get Vermont back on track and growing again. He is ready to tackle affordability issues around housing, education and the cost of living. A veteran Legislator known for his decency, fairness and consensus building skills, he creates common ground in the Statehouse to get things done. A small business owner, he understands firsthand that sector's pressures.” Liz Ruffa, East Dorset(Source: Manchester Journal: ) ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS Burlington Free Press: Vermont aging faster than the rest of the U.S. Burlington Free Press Columnist and UVM Associate Professor of Economics Art Woolf analyzed new data this week indicating that Vermont’s population is aging more quickly than the rest of the U.S. Woolf noted that Vermont’s median age is now 42.8 – rising by 10 years since 1990, while the national median has risen only five years during that same time. Read the full article . Phil has made it clear that solving our demographic challenges is critical to growing our economy. That’s why his plan to grow the economy focuses on rebuilding the working-age population so we can grow our workforce and reduce our individual tax burden. Read that plan . WCAX: Vt. Kids learn police tactics from the pros Phil often talks about a civic duty and our shared responsibility to give back to society in whatever way speaks to us most – whether that’s through public service, military service, law enforcement or emergency response, community building, volunteering, etc. Educating our kids on the opportunities for fulfilling that civic duty is more important than ever. WCAX highlighted a unique program from the Northfield Police Department – a great example of how we can engage our youth and give them a sense of service. Read the full article and watch Phil discuss civic duty . Phil and Team Scott are working very hard to share Phil’s positive messages across the state. If you like what our campaign stands for, last week it was reported that our Primary opponent is well on his way to spending $2,000,000 dollars – of his own money - and he’s using that money on misleading, dishonest, negative ads. Please help Phil and the team run a positive campaign, one that our children can be proud of! Thanks to everyone that came out to events and participated in meetings this week. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities everyday – and share your thoughts – and . > Friend,
>
> With night after night of backwards and bigoted rhetoric at the Republican National Convention, last night truly took the cake when we finally had a chance to hear from the official Republican presidential candidate: Donald J. Trump.
>
> It was everything we expected and so much worse. Instead of taking a moment on the national stage to talk about a positive vision of moving our country forward, he doubled down on extremist language and employed the worst kind of fear-mongering to try to get Americans to forget how totally unqualified he is to be our president and govern this country. It was unsettling and disgusting.
>
> Mostly, though, it's terrifying. But it left no doubt that we cannot let this man become our next president. **[Please chip in right now to help us fight back against Donald Trump and his allies in Washington.>>> ](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91a64/70cdc2ba/47074e5f/397038d/2857466522/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh-trump-convention?refcode=em072216&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+FIRSTNAMEPlease+chip+in+right+now+to+hel&utm_campaign=em072116_2&source=em072116_2")**

| Dear Friend, I thought that Donald Trump couldn't sink any lower. I thought that his racism, his discriminatory remarks, and his explicit intent to divide Americans was enough. Until Donald Trump said this during the GOP convention in Cleveland: | **" The irresponsible rhetoric of our President, who has used the pulpit of the presidency to divide us by race and color, has made America a more dangerous environment that frankly that I have ever seen of anybody in this room has ever watched or seen."**
---
Inexcusable. And you know what else is inexcusable? Our opponent, right here in Washington state, has spent over a year refusing to come clean with voters about Trump and their shared values. While Trump spews hate, our opponent stands silent. It's shameful, it's not leadership, and it's certainly not worthy of our great state.
I won't allow Donald Trump's style of hatred and division to come to Washington state. I won't allow Republicans Donald Trump and Bill Bryant to halt the progress we are making as a state.
[**Will you contribute $5 to my campaign today and send a message to our opponent about his refusal to stand up to Trump?**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=OTI0NDY3MgS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYxNzk3NjkzS0&mt=2&rj=OTYxNzk3NjkzS0&rt=0)

Jay Inslee

We have only 109 days left. | Friend --

I’m heading home from Cleveland today -- it’s still sinking in that this week really happened.

The Republicans really spent four days saying Hillary should be in jail and chanting “lock her up.”

Donald Trump really won the Republican nomination.

And we really have just 109 days left to beat him.

If you’ve been waiting to get involved, if you’ve been hoping that somehow this would play out differently, if you’ve been relying on someone else to do the work it takes to help Hillary win, that has to stop right now.

If we don’t all step up, we could lose. And I saw this week in Cleveland just how terrifying a prospect that is.

**Don’t wait another second -- with just 109 days left, every dollar counts. Chip in right now, and let’s get to work making sure Hillary is our next president (and we’ll send you a sticker as a thank you!):** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=8&ms=MjYwNTQ3NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQzMjM0NTMzS0&mt=1&rt=0)

clean_body: We have only 109 days left. | Friend -- I’m heading home from Cleveland today -- it’s still sinking in that this week really happened. The Republicans really spent four days saying Hillary should be in jail and chanting “lock her up.” Donald Trump really won the Republican nomination. And we really have just 109 days left to beat him. If you’ve been waiting to get involved, if you’ve been hoping that somehow this would play out differently, if you’ve been relying on someone else to do the work it takes to help Hillary win, that has to stop right now. If we don’t all step up, we could lose. And I saw this week in Cleveland just how terrifying a prospect that is. Don’t wait another second -- with just 109 days left, every dollar counts.
